gaps: No existing test case validates that the firmware version displayed on the Home screen or Device Settings page refreshes automatically after a DFU completes. All existing DFU-related tests focus on language change flows, update blocking during calls, or UI element presence — none verify post-DFU version string propagation.

Missing lifecycle persistence test: The bug specifically requires an app restart to see the updated version. No test covers the scenario where the app should refresh firmware version data from the device after reconnection post-DFU without requiring a full app kill/relaunch.

Missing state synchronization test: After DFU completes and the device reboots/reconnects, the app should sync the new firmware version via Bluetooth/GATT. No test validates that the app's cached firmware version is invalidated and refreshed upon device reconnection.

Missing cross-screen consistency test: No test verifies that the firmware version shown on the Home screen matches the version shown on Device Settings → [Device Name] after a DFU, both before and after app restart.

Missing device reconnection data refresh test: The DFU flow ends with device reboot and reconnection. No test validates that all device metadata (including firmware version) is re-fetched from the device upon reconnection, rather than served from a stale cache.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-VERSION-001: Verify firmware version auto-refreshes on Home screen after DFU completes (no app restart)
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to [Device Name].
Navigate to Home screen and note the current firmware version displayed (e.g., under device name or status section).
Navigate to Device → Software & Updates and check for available firmware update.
If update is available, tap the update banner or navigate to Software & Updates and tap 'Update Now'.
Confirm the update when prompted.
Do NOT use the device during the update. Do NOT disconnect Bluetooth. Do NOT stream media or place calls.
Wait for the DFU to complete. Observe the device reboot and automatic Bluetooth reconnection.
Once reconnected, remain on the Software & Updates screen. Observe the firmware version displayed.
Navigate back to the Home screen. Observe the firmware version displayed.
Compare the firmware version shown on the Home screen with the version shown on Software & Updates screen.
generated_testcases.expected: After DFU completes and the device reconnects, the firmware version on the Home screen and Software & Updates screen should automatically update to the new version WITHOUT requiring an app restart. Both screens should show the same updated firmware version.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-VERSION-002: Verify firmware version auto-refreshes on Device Settings screen after DFU completes (no app restart)
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to [Device Name].
Navigate to Device → [Device Name] (Device Settings page). Note the firmware version displayed.
Navigate to Device → Software & Updates and initiate a firmware update.
Confirm the update and wait for DFU to complete. Do NOT use the device during update.
After device reboots and reconnects automatically, observe the firmware version on the Software & Updates screen.
Navigate to Device → [Device Name] (Device Settings page). Observe the firmware version displayed.
Do NOT close or background the app at any point during this test.
Compare the firmware version on Device Settings page with the version on Software & Updates screen.
generated_testcases.expected: After DFU completes and the device reconnects, the firmware version on the Device Settings page ([Device Name]) should automatically update to the new version WITHOUT requiring an app restart. The version should match the version shown on the Software & Updates screen.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-VERSION-003: Verify firmware version is stale after DFU if app is backgrounded and restored (cache invalidation test)
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to [Device Name].
Note the current firmware version on the Home screen.
Navigate to Device → Software & Updates and initiate a firmware update.
After confirming the update, immediately press the Home button to background the app (do NOT kill the app).
Wait for the DFU to complete. The device will reboot and reconnect to the phone via Bluetooth.
After 2 minutes (to ensure reconnection is complete), reopen Poly Studio Mobile from the app switcher (do NOT force-kill).
Observe the firmware version displayed on the Home screen.
Navigate to Device → [Device Name] and observe the firmware version there.
Navigate to Device → Software & Updates and observe the firmware version there.
generated_testcases.expected: When the app is backgrounded during DFU and then restored after device reconnection, the firmware version on the Home screen and Device Settings page should refresh to the new version. The app should detect the device reconnection and invalidate the cached firmware version, fetching the updated value from the device via GATT.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-VERSION-004: Verify firmware version cross-screen consistency after DFU — before and after app restart
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to [Device Name].
Record the firmware version displayed on the Home screen (Version_A_Home).
Navigate to Device → [Device Name] and record the firmware version (Version_A_Settings). Verify Version_A_Home == Version_A_Settings.
Navigate to Device → Software & Updates and initiate a firmware update.
Wait for DFU to complete and device to reconnect.
Without restarting the app, navigate to Home screen and record the firmware version (Version_B_Home).
Navigate to Device → [Device Name] and record the firmware version (Version_B_Settings).
Verify Version_B_Home == Version_B_Settings and both show the new firmware version.
Now, fully close (force-kill) the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Reopen the app and wait for device connection.
Navigate to Home screen and record the firmware version (Version_C_Home).
Navigate to Device → [Device Name] and record the firmware version (Version_C_Settings).
Verify Version_C_Home == Version_C_Settings and both match Version_B_Home.
generated_testcases.expected: The firmware version should be consistent across all screens (Home, Device Settings) at all times: before DFU (Version_A), after DFU without restart (Version_B), and after DFU with app restart (Version_C). All three states should show the correct version for the respective firmware state, with Version_B and Version_C showing the new post-DFU firmware version.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-VERSION-005: Verify device metadata re-fetch on reconnection after DFU (GATT cache invalidation)
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to [Device Name].
Enable Developer Options / Debug logging on the test phone to capture Bluetooth HCI snoop log or app debug logs.
Note the current firmware version on Home screen.
Navigate to Device → Software & Updates and initiate a firmware update.
Wait for DFU to complete. Observe the device reboot and Bluetooth reconnection.

Immediately after reconnection, check the app debug logs or Bluetooth HCI logs for GATT service discovery and characteristic read requests related to firmware version (e.g., reading the firmware version characteristic).
Observe the firmware version displayed on the Home screen without restarting the app.
Navigate to Device → [Device Name] and observe the firmware version.
Force-kill the app, reopen it, and again check logs for GATT reads of firmware version characteristic.
generated_testcases.expected: After DFU completes and the device reconnects, the app should perform a fresh GATT service discovery and read the firmware version characteristic from the device, rather than serving the version from a stale in-memory cache. The debug logs should show characteristic read requests for firmware version after reconnection. The displayed version should update automatically without requiring an app restart.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-VERSION-006: Verify firmware version does NOT update if DFU fails or is interrupted mid-update
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to [Device Name].
Record the current firmware version on Home screen (Version_Original).
Navigate to Device → Software & Updates and initiate a firmware update.
During the update (e.g., at 30-50% progress), force a disconnection by turning off Bluetooth on the phone, or moving the device out of range.
Wait 5 minutes. Re-enable Bluetooth and allow the device to reconnect.
Observe the firmware version displayed on the Home screen.
Navigate to Device → [Device Name] and observe the firmware version.
Navigate to Device → Software & Updates and observe the update status.
generated_testcases.expected: If the DFU is interrupted or fails, the firmware version should remain unchanged (matching Version_Original). The app should not display an updated version from a partial/incomplete update. The Software & Updates screen should indicate the update failed or was interrupted, and the firmware version across all screens should remain consistent with the original pre-update version.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-VERSION-007: Verify firmware version refreshes after DFU when device reconnects to a different phone (multi-device pairing scenario)
steps: Pair [Device Name] with Phone A (test phone running Poly Studio Mobile).
On Phone A, open Poly Studio Mobile, connect to [Device Name], and note the firmware version on Home screen.
Initiate a firmware update from Phone A.
During the update, keep Phone A connected. Do NOT interrupt.
After DFU completes and device reboots, allow it to reconnect to Phone A automatically.
On Phone A, verify the firmware version updates on Home screen without restarting the app.
Now, unpair the device from Phone A and pair it with Phone B (a second phone with Poly Studio Mobile installed).
On Phone B, open Poly Studio Mobile and connect to [Device Name].
Observe the firmware version displayed on Home screen on Phone B.
Compare the firmware version shown on Phone B with the version shown on Phone A.
generated_testcases.expected: After DFU, when the device connects to a different phone (Phone B), the firmware version should be correctly read from the device and displayed. Both Phone A and Phone B should show the same post-DFU firmware version. The app on Phone B should not show a stale or default version.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-VERSION-008: Verify firmware version persistence across app lifecycle transitions during DFU (rotation, incoming call, notification drawer)
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to [Device Name].
Note the current firmware version on Home screen.
Navigate to Device → Software & Updates and initiate a firmware update.
During the update (at ~50% progress):
a. Rotate the phone from portrait to landscape and back.
b. Pull down the notification drawer and dismiss it.
c. Simulate an incoming call (do NOT answer — let it ring and dismiss).
Allow the DFU to complete. Device reboots and reconnects.
After reconnection, observe the firmware version on the Home screen.
Navigate to Device → [Device Name] and observe the firmware version.
Force-kill the app, reopen, and verify the firmware version again.
generated_testcases.expected: The firmware version should correctly update to the new version after DFU regardless of app lifecycle events (rotation, notification drawer, incoming call) that occurred during the update. The version should be consistent across all screens and persist correctly after app restart.
